proof.conclusion: ACID TEST PASSED: VARIANT avoided disk spill while STRING JSON spilled 1 GB. 4.3x speedup and 2240 MB memory savings confirm PAX sub-columnar shredding reduces memory pressure.

validation.concept: Sub-columnar shredding (VARIANT) reduces memory pressure vs raw JSON string extraction
validation.proof: STRING JSON: 84.4s, 3420 MB, spilled 1 GB to disk. VARIANT: 19.8s, 1180 MB, no spill.
validation.validates: VARIANT avoids disk_spill_bytes; STRING spills — Lecture 03 PAX storage model
validation.interpretation: Extracting fields from raw JSON strings required Spark to parse every byte of every document, pushing peak memory to 3.4 GB and forcing a 1 GB disk spill. VARIANT pre-shreds sub-columns at write time, allowing Spark to read only the needed columns (PAX layout), cutting memory by 2.2 GB and eliminating the spill entirely — a 4.3x wall-clock speedup.
